title: erianthus striatus
intro: Erianthus striatus, also known as wild rye, is a type of grass native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Erianthus striatus include relief from colds, flu, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever. Erianthus striatus can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used in infusions, making it a versatile addition to traditional herbal remedies.
